frame_analyses.description: Educational presentation title slide about suturing and knot-tying for EMIG exam
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This is educational content, not a performance

frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer setup visible with marked Penrose drain on red platform, suture already threaded through tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS Task 5 setup - marked Penrose drain with black targeting marks visible

frame_analyses.description: Right-hand instrument grasping suture to begin first throw of knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument positioning for knot tying initiation

frame_analyses.description: Creating loops for surgeon's knot with right hand, left instrument stabilizing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be constructing a surgeon's knot with multiple wraps on first throw

frame_analyses.description: Completing the surgeon's knot formation with both instruments engaged
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bilateral instrument coordination during knot construction

frame_analyses.description: Tightening the first throw, knot appears secure against tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw being properly seated and tightened

frame_analyses.description: Knot completed and secured, tissue appears well-approximated
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot appears secure with good tissue approximation

frame_analyses.description: Running stitch demonstration setup with long marked Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Different task setup - this is for running stitch demonstration

frame_analyses.description: Needle being loaded and positioned for running suture technique
Sequential suture placement along the Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Running suture technique with consistent bite spacing

frame_analyses.description: Cutting suture tail after completion of running suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final step of running suture completion

drain_assessment.assessment_note: This is an educational demonstration video showing multiple techniques rather than a timed performance

technique_summary: This is an educational presentation demonstrating proper intracorporeal knot tying and running suture techniques. The video shows instructional content rather than a timed performance assessment.
improvement_suggestions: This is educational content - no performance assessment applicable
strengths: Clear demonstration of proper surgical techniques
Good educational value for learning intracorporeal suturing
